steps.title: Find the general claim
steps.content: The narrator begins with a broad observation about boating: each member of the crew believes that he is doing everything. The rest of the passage supports that claim with the specific situation involving Harris and George. Harris thinks he alone has been working, while George thinks Harris has done little and that George himself has done the labor worth mentioning.

steps.title: Reject narrower purposes
steps.content:
• Choice A calls the situation unexpected, but the narrator says he has "always noticed" this pattern.
  • Choice B focuses on the narrator's confidence about his own role, which is not the passage's main purpose.
  • Choice C suggests the text examines actual contributions to a challenge, but the passage emphasizes each person's belief about who worked.
The remaining option captures the generalization and the supporting examples. The answer is D.
